channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Against all odds, this aquarium has reared a very special kind of fish in captivity
item.description: Staff at Chicago's Shedd Aquarium have reared a special kind of fish known as a warty frogfish for the first time in captivity. Their success may hold broader lessons for raising marine species.
item.content:encoded: Staff at Chicago's Shedd Aquarium have reared a special kind of fish known as a warty frogfish for the first time in captivity. Their success may hold broader lessons for raising marine species.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Why it's so hard to take down Cambodia's online scam industry
item.description: Americans lost some $10 billion to online scammers in Southeast Asia in 2024. Cambodia, in particular, has been warned to clean up its act in recent months.
item.content:encoded: Americans lost some $10 billion to online scammers in Southeast Asia in 2024. Cambodia, in particular, has been warned to clean up its act in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Michael Sullivan

item.title: A former TSA security chief weighs in on the chaos at U.S. airports
item.description: TSA staffing shortages have led to hours-long lines for travelers at many airports. Keith Jeffries, the former TSA security director at Los Angeles International Airport shares insights on the crisis.
item.content:encoded: TSA staffing shortages have led to hours-long lines for travelers at many airports. Keith Jeffries, the former TSA security director at Los Angeles International Airport shares insights on the crisis.
item.dc:creator: Jordan-Marie Smith

item.title: These families help scientists find Alzheimer's treatments. Their network is at risk
item.description: A network of families riddled with Alzheimer's has given scientists a unique window on the disease. But the group's future is uncertain amid funding cuts by the Trump Administration.
item.content:encoded: A network of families riddled with Alzheimer's has given scientists a unique window on the disease. But the group's future is uncertain amid funding cuts by the Trump Administration.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Sailing season kicks off with a sock-burning party in Annapolis, Md.
item.description: In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by making a sacrifice – of their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.
item.content:encoded: In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by making a sacrifice – of their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: How money is changing March Madness
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Dan Wolken, senior sports writer with Yahoo Sports, about the the culprits responsible for the lack of Cinderella teams in the past two years' NCAA basketball Sweet 16s.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Dan Wolken, senior sports writer with Yahoo Sports, about the the culprits responsible for the lack of Cinderella teams in the past two years' NCAA basketball Sweet 16s.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: How a baseball coach made a teenager feel seen
item.description: Dan Roche, who was born with a cataract that left him mostly blind in one eye, was on a baseball team at age 15. On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, he shares a memory of his coach.
item.content:encoded: Dan Roche, who was born with a cataract that left him mostly blind in one eye, was on a baseball team at age 15. On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, he shares a memory of his coach.

item.title: Despite state bans and restrictions, the number of abortions in the U.S. holds steady
item.description: A new report shows the number of abortions has held steady in recent years despite efforts to limit access.
item.content:encoded: A new report shows the number of abortions has held steady in recent years despite efforts to limit access.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Food delivery drivers are being squeezed by higher gas prices
item.description: The effects of the war in Iran are far-reaching, extending to drivers for DoorDash and Uber Eats. They're paying more for gas — and being squeezed by competition. DoorDash is rolling out some relief.
item.content:encoded: The effects of the war in Iran are far-reaching, extending to drivers for DoorDash and Uber Eats. They're paying more for gas — and being squeezed by competition. DoorDash is rolling out some relief.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: 'We're all in grief': Chicano artists reckon with Cesar Chavez abuse allegations
item.description: Artists who helped build Cesar Chavez's legacy are now grappling with allegations of sexual assault against the late labor leader.
item.content:encoded: Artists who helped build Cesar Chavez's legacy are now grappling with allegations of sexual assault against the late labor leader.
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco
